crypto-cgt.methodology_markdown: The local engine refuses to calculate unless an individual confirms that the disposal has already been classified on capital account. Proceeds or fiat market value less acquisition cost and direct acquisition and disposal costs produces the transaction gain or loss. Nigeria calculates incremental individual tax using the Nigeria Tax Act 2025 bands after other chargeable income. Kenya and Ghana apply 15% only to a confirmed in-scope capital gain. South Africa aggregates current gains and losses, applies the R50,000 natural-person annual exclusion and assessed losses, includes 40% of the net capital gain, and calculates incremental individual tax. Unsupported dates, jurisdictions, taxpayer types, trading, business, mining, staking, rewards and uncertain classifications fail closed.
known_limitations: The tool does not decide whether activity is capital or revenue; that classification must already be confirmed.
Companies, trusts, non-residents, deceased estates, business trading, mining, staking, airdrops, rewards and compensation are excluded.
Lot selection, exchange rates, valuation disputes, wallet imports, provider-fee excise, VAT, penalties, interest, filing and payment deadlines are not calculated.
The estimate and local exports are not a return, assessment, tax opinion, filing instruction or cost-basis record.

ng-cgt.methodology_markdown: The shared local engine calculates the non-negative gain as disposal proceeds less acquisition cost or tax residue and qualifying disposal costs. For individuals it calculates the incremental Fourth Schedule tax after other chargeable income using the 0%, 15%, 18%, 21%, 23% and 25% bands. For companies it applies 0% only when turnover, fixed-assets and professional-services tests all qualify; otherwise it applies 30%. Nigerian-share threshold relief requires aggregate proceeds below NGN 150 million and aggregate gains no more than NGN 10 million in 12 consecutive months; same-year reinvestment is apportioned. Principal-residence relief is applied only after the user confirms the scoped statutory conditions.
known_limitations: Part disposals, valuation disputes, capital-allowance residue disputes, gifts, compensation, insurance and personal-chattel rules are not calculated.
Non-resident indirect transfers, trusts, restructurings and transaction-specific exemptions require a dedicated computation and professional review.
The estimate is not a return, assessment, filing instruction, tax-clearance document or substitute for NRS, state-tax-authority or professional advice.

za-uif.law_or_version: UIF contribution ceiling effective 2021-06-01; Unemployment Insurance Amendment Act 2016 and current Department benefit guidance reviewed 2026-07-22
za-uif.methodology_markdown: Contributions apply 1% employee and 1% employer to monthly UIF-liable remuneration capped at ZAR 17,712. Ordinary planning uses daily income equal to the lower of average monthly remuneration and ZAR 17,712, multiplied by 12 and divided by 365. The income replacement percentage is 29.2 plus 7,173.92 divided by 232.92 plus daily income, bounded to 38%-60% for days 1-238; verified credit days 239-365 use 20%. Maternity is calculated separately at 66% of the capped benefit basis for at most 121 days, then limited so employer pay plus UIF does not exceed the actual normal remuneration entered.
known_limitations: Planning estimate only; UIF determines eligibility, verified credits, claim dates, approved days and payment.
Credit days must come from an official UIF record or confirmation; the calculator does not infer them from employment dates.
Adoption, parental, commissioning-parent, illness, dependant and reduced-work-time benefits are guidance-only and receive no amount estimate.
The calculator is not a claim submission, employer declaration, legal opinion or promise of processing time.

It then applies 0% or 30% CIT to user-entered total profits and 0% or 4% development levy to separately entered assessable profits. It flags section 57 review for an MNE constituent or aggregate turnover of at least NGN 20 billion but does not invent an effective-tax-rate top-up.
known_limitations: The user must obtain total profits and assessable profits from reviewed accounts or an adviser; the app does not derive either tax base.
Minimum effective tax, losses, incentives, transitional adjustments and specialised sectors are not calculated.
The estimate is not a return, assessment, filing instruction or substitute for NRS guidance or professional advice.

VAT withholding is not automatically calculated because designation, supplier or supply eligibility, and current exemption status must all be confirmed.
UGX 150 million is shown as an annual reference; the compulsory-registration screen follows URA's strict past- and expected-three-month tests.

cbk-rates.verified_by: AfroTools Day 3 source, privacy and browser review
cbk-rates.law_or_version: CBK public table methodology and manual-input contract reviewed 2026-07-22; AfroTools does not ingest, cache or bundle the table values.
cbk-rates.methodology_markdown: The user opens the official CBK table and enters a dated Mean rate expressed as KES per one or one hundred foreign-currency units. The local-only calculation is KES = foreign amount divided by quoted units multiplied by the entered CBK Mean rate. Currency must be a three-letter code, all numeric inputs must be positive, the source date is required and cannot be in the future, and inverse KES-per-regional-currency rows are unsupported.
known_limitations: The tool supplies no current, latest, live, bundled, cached or fallback rate and does not verify the user's transcription.
CBK values are indicative market-opening averages, not executable bank, bureau, card, wallet or remittance quotes.
Provider spreads, fees, liquidity, settlement timing and inverse CBK rows are not modeled.
No PDF, export, rate storage, cloud upload or network calculation is provided.

change_history.note: Replaced an unsafe third-party live-rate concept with a multilingual local-only converter using user-entered official CBK Mean rate, quoted unit and source date.

source_titles: IOPS Good practices for pension projections
pension-proj.methodology_markdown: The local engine grows the opening balance monthly using the equivalent monthly rate derived from the user-entered gross annual return minus annual fee drag, then adds that month's personal, employer and voluntary contributions. Contributions change at the start of each projection year using the entered annual growth assumption. Today's-money value discounts the ending balance using entered inflation. Lower and higher cases change gross return by two percentage points and are deterministic sensitivities, not probabilities.
known_limitations: No country contribution rate, earnings ceiling, tax relief, retirement age, access rule, provider return or inflation forecast is supplied.
The engine does not calculate a defined-benefit entitlement, state pension, annuity, drawdown, safe withdrawal, tax result or currency conversion.
The result is not a fund quote, provider ranking, legal opinion, financial advice or guaranteed retirement outcome.
AfroTools AI may route to the page but cannot request, prefill, retain or receive pension balances, contribution amounts, source evidence or identifiers.

change_history.note: Removed unsupported country presets, Monte Carlo claims, PFA rankings, annuity, drawdown, tax-relief and silent AI-send paths; replaced them with an evidence-gated deterministic local projection.

source_titles: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis - The Fisher Equation
investment-return.methodology_markdown: The engine converts the user-entered nominal annual return and selected annual compounding frequency into an equivalent monthly rate. It projects an initial investment and monthly contributions at the selected beginning- or end-of-month timing. Effective annual return is derived from the nominal rate and compounding frequency. Real annual return uses (1 + effective return) / (1 + inflation) - 1, and purchasing-power value discounts the final projected balance by the entered inflation assumption. Gain on contributions is not labelled CAGR when intermediate monthly cash flows exist.
known_limitations: The entered return and inflation rates are assumptions, not forecasts, provider quotes or live market data.
Fees, taxes, volatility, defaults, liquidity limits, missed contributions and currency movement are not modelled.
Currency selection changes display formatting only and performs no exchange-rate conversion.
The result is not investment advice, a suitability assessment or a guaranteed return.

change_history.note: Replaced inline and widget formulas with one reviewed engine; corrected cash-flow CAGR labelling, compounding frequency, contribution timing, real return, privacy and source boundaries.
